# Create static LDAP roles

Configure static roles to map names in Vault to LDAP entries and use the
rotation settings to manage credentials automatically with the role.

<Tip title="Assumptions">

- You have [set up an `ldap` plugin](/vault/docs/secrets/ldap/setup). 
- You have permission to update roles in Vault.
- You have permission to make `POST` calls to the `ldap` plugin.

</Tip>

1. Create a configuration file, `ldap-role.json` with your role settings:

   @include 'ldap/example-config/static-role/basic.mdx'

1. Save the new role.
   <Tabs>

   <Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

   @include 'ldap/create-static-role/cli.mdx'

   </Tab>

   <Tab heading="API" group="api">

   @include 'ldap/create-static-role/api.mdx'

   </Tab>

   </Tabs>

1. Confirm the role settings by requesting credentials from the plugin:

   <Tabs>

   <Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

   @include 'ldap/get-role-creds/cli.mdx'

   </Tab>

   <Tab heading="API" group="api">

   @include 'ldap/get-role-creds/api.mdx'

   </Tab>

   </Tabs>


## Rotate credentials on read

@include 'alerts/enterprise-only.mdx'

When enabled, Vault rotates the static role credential on reads from
`/{mount_path}/static-cred/{role_name}`, subject to a configurable cooldown
period. Rotate-on-read is independent of any schedule-based rotation configured
for the role. A cooldown period prevents redundant rotations when multiple
clients read the same role in quick succession.

The credential response includes `rotated_on_read: true` when a read triggers
a rotation, or `rotated_on_read: false` when the credential is still within the
cooldown window.

Rotate-on-read can be configured at the mount level (applies to all static
roles) or overridden at the role level:

- `rotate_on_read` `(bool: false)` - When `true`, credentials are rotated on
  reads, subject to the cooldown period. Set in `ldap.json` for the mount, or
  in `ldap-role.json` to override per role.
- `rotate_on_read_cooldown` `(duration: "1m")` - Minimum time between
  rotations. The existing credential is returned for reads within the cooldown
  window. Set in `ldap.json` for the mount, or in `ldap-role.json` to override
  per role.

<Warning>

Setting `rotate_on_read_cooldown` to `0` disables the cooldown entirely.
Every read will trigger a credential rotation, which can cause excessive load
on your LDAP server when multiple clients read the same role simultaneously.

</Warning>

Mount-level example (`ldap.json`):

<CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

```json
{
  "rotate_on_read":          true,
  "rotate_on_read_cooldown": "5m"
}
```

</CodeBlockConfig>

Role-level override example (`ldap-role.json`):

<CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

```json
{
  "rotate_on_read":          true,
  "rotate_on_read_cooldown": "10m"
}
```

</CodeBlockConfig>
